sections.heading: Agent Bridge

items.description: Exact skill name returned by list_agent_skills.

rows: Load an agent skill's instructions. Parameter name must be the exact skill name returned by list_agent_skills; use before tasks that need that specialized guidance.
none
Return agent bridge configuration status, discovered skills, configured MCP servers, and load errors.

items.description: JSON object of arguments passed to the upstream MCP tool.
Exact configured agent MCP server name.
Exact upstream tool name exposed by the selected agent MCP server.

rows: Call a tool on a configured agent MCP server. Parameters: server and tool must match list_agent_mcp_tools; args is a JSON object matching that tool schema, or empty for no-argument tools.
none
List configured agent MCP servers. Use to find exact server names and connection status before listing or calling bridged MCP tools.

items.description: Optional exact agent MCP server name to filter listed tools.

rows: List tools exposed by configured agent MCP servers. Parameter server is optional; omit it for all servers or pass an exact server name before call_agent_mcp_tool.
none
List agent skills discovered from config. Use to find the exact skill name before activate_agent_skill; this only lists available instruction sets and does not load them.

sections.heading: Bash

items.description: Whether to start long-running non-interactive work as a tracked background job owned by this session.
Shell command string to execute for terminal work such as tests, builds, package managers, git, or scripts.
Optional working directory for the command, resolved inside the agent session workdir. Omit or pass . to use the session workdir.
Optional environment variables for multiline or quote-heavy values; reference them from the command.
Optional combined stdout/stderr byte budget for bounded command mode.
Optional name for the tracked job or persistent shell.
Whether to start the command in a persistent PTY shell for interactive programs, REPLs, servers, or commands needing later input. PTY mode returns shell_id for persistent-shell companion tools.
Optional short purpose explaining why this tool call is being made. Maximum 500 characters.
8-character alphanumeric agent/workspace session_id returned by session_start.
Optional timeout in seconds for bounded command mode.

rows: Run terminal commands inside an explicit agent/workspace session for builds, tests, package managers, git inspection, scripts, and other shell work. Pass the session_id returned by session_start. cwd defaults to the session workdir; any cwd override resolves inside that session workdir. Prefer the `cwd` argument over embedded directory changes; use `env` for multiline or quote-heavy values. Default mode is bounded and returns captured stdout/stderr. Set `async_=true` for long-running non-interactive work; this returns a job_id owned by the same session_id and must be managed with the `job` companion. Set `pty=true` only for interactive programs, REPLs, servers, or commands that need later input; this returns a shell_id for persistent-shell companion tools. Do not use shell_id with `job`, and do not use session_id with persistent-shell companion tools. If both async_ and pty are true, PTY mode is used. Use built-in `search` for source/content discovery so output carries edit-grounding metadata. Current bounded command timeout default/cap: 10/60 seconds.

sections.body: Register protected tools for creating and managing download links.
sections.heading: Downloads

items.description: Optional browser download filename. Path components are stripped.
Optional maximum number of downloads. Use 0 for unlimited; omit to use the configured default.
Existing regular file path to expose through a temporary tokenized download URL.
8-character alphanumeric agent/workspace session_id returned by session_start.
Optional link lifetime in seconds. Omit to use the configured default; values above the configured cap are clamped.

rows: Create a temporary tokenized HTTP download URL for one existing regular file in a local agent/workspace session. Use this when a user needs to download or open an artifact through a browser. The response includes a sensitive token and URL. Current TTL default/cap: 3600/604800 seconds. Current file-size cap: 0 bytes, with 0 meaning no configured cap.

items.description: Whether to include expired or exhausted links in the listing.
8-character alphanumeric agent/workspace session_id returned by session_start.

rows: List tokenized file download links created by this session.

items.description: 8-character alphanumeric agent/workspace session_id returned by session_start.
Sensitive download token returned by create_file_link or list_file_links.

rows: Revoke a tokenized file download link created by this session.

sections.heading: File

items.description: Workspace-relative path, or an allowed absolute path, for the file or directory operation.
Whether to recurse into descendant directories. Required for deleting non-empty directories.
8-character alphanumeric agent/workspace session_id returned by session_start.

rows: Delete a file or directory inside a session workdir.

items.description: 1-based final original line to replace. The range is inclusive.
Workspace-relative path, or an allowed absolute path, for the file or directory operation.
Replacement text for the selected whole-line range. Use an empty string to delete the range.
8-character alphanumeric agent/workspace session_id returned by session_start.
Optional snapshot_id returned by read or search. When provided, the edit is rejected if the file changed or the line range was not shown.
1-based first original line to replace. The range is inclusive.

rows: Replace an inclusive 1-based whole-line range in a UTF-8 file, grounded by a recent read/search snapshot. Prefer this for normal code edits after `read` or `search` displayed the target lines. Pass `snapshot_id` from the grounding result and the same `session_id` when present. `replacement` is the final content for the range. Keep ranges tight, edit only displayed lines, and use the fresh numbered context returned by a successful edit before the next edit. Current write cap: 5000000 bytes.

items.description: Maximum number of entries to return before reporting truncation. Bounded by the server configuration.
Directory path to list. Relative paths resolve inside the agent/workspace session workdir.
Whether to recurse into descendant directories. Required for deleting non-empty directories.
8-character alphanumeric agent/workspace session_id returned by session_start.

rows: List files and directories under a session workdir path for quick inspection. Relative paths resolve inside the explicit agent/workspace session. The result reports whether entries were truncated by the requested limit or server cap. Current max directory entries: 5000.

items.description: Complete UTF-8 text content to write to the target file.
Whether to replace an existing file. Set false to fail when the target already exists.
Workspace-relative path, or an allowed absolute path, for the file or directory operation.
8-character alphanumeric agent/workspace session_id returned by session_start.

rows: Write a complete UTF-8 file inside an explicit agent/workspace session. Use for new files or intentional whole-file replacement. For precise modifications to existing files, prefer grounded `edit_lines`; use patch tools only when clearer. Do not replace an existing file wholesale unless that is the intended edit. Current write cap: 5000000 bytes.

sections.body: Register bash async job companion tools.
sections.heading: Jobs

items.description: Tracked bash job ids in this session to stop because they are stalled, hung, or no longer needed.
Whether to include terminal jobs with exited, stopped, or lost status. Set false to show only active jobs.
Number of recent terminal lines to capture for a tracked job. Output is available only while the background job can still be inspected.
Whether to return a snapshot of tracked bash jobs owned by this session. Omit all other job actions to list by default.
Tracked bash job ids in this session whose latest output/status should be inspected. Use only when you need to check specific async bash work.
Tracked bash job ids in this session to restart with their original command and working directory.
8-character alphanumeric agent/workspace session_id returned by session_start.

rows:
Inspect, read output from, stop, or retry async bash jobs owned by an explicit agent session.

Results for `bash(async_=true)` are tracked by job id under the same session_id. Reach for this companion tool only when you need to intervene: list running work in the session, inspect recent output, stop stalled work, or retry a finished command. Starting work belongs in `bash`, not `job`.

Pass the session_id from session_start. Call with no action, or with `list_jobs=true`, to list jobs owned by that session. Use `poll=[id]` to inspect recent output/status for specific jobs, `cancel=[id]` to stop jobs, or `retry=[id]` to restart jobs with their original command and working directory. Do not combine actions in one call.

sections.heading: Patch

items.description: Working directory where patch paths are resolved. Relative paths resolve inside the agent/workspace session workdir.
Unified diff text to validate and apply with git apply.
8-character alphanumeric agent/workspace session_id returned by session_start.

rows: Apply a unified diff inside an explicit agent/workspace session.

sections.body: Register the read tool with selector support.
sections.heading: Read

items.description: File or directory path with optional selector suffix. Examples: file.py, file.py:50, file.py:50-80, file.py:50+20, file.py:raw, file.py:50-80:raw.
8-character alphanumeric agent/workspace session_id returned by session_start.

rows: Read files or list directories with optional selector suffixes in the path. Use this for normal code context. Put ranges in the path selector and keep numbered output for edit grounding. File output includes snapshot metadata for edit_lines. Supported selectors: path:50, path:50-80, path:50+20, path:raw, and path:50-80:raw. Current per-file read cap: 512000 bytes.

sections.heading: Remote

items.description: Remote control-plane action: invite, list, revoke, or rename workers.
Action-specific control-plane arguments. invite accepts name, workdir, ttl_s; list accepts {}; revoke accepts machine; rename accepts machine and new_name.

rows: Run compact remote control-plane actions. Use action="list" to discover worker names; action="invite" to create a one-time join command; action="revoke" to remove a stale or untrusted worker; and action="rename" to give a worker a stable name. Defaults: invite ttl_s defaults to the configured remote_invite_ttl_s=600 seconds when omitted. Security: treat invite output as sensitive because it grants enrollment capability.

sections.heading: Search

items.description: Directory path that narrows the search root. Relative paths resolve inside the agent/workspace session workdir.
Maximum number of matching paths to return. Bounded by server configuration.
Glob expression matched against workspace-relative paths and file names.
8-character alphanumeric agent/workspace session_id returned by session_start.

rows: Find files by glob pattern inside an explicit agent/workspace session when you know filename patterns and need matching paths, not file contents. Pass the session_id returned by session_start. cwd defaults to the session workdir; any relative cwd override resolves inside that session workdir. Remote sessions dispatch to the paired worker session. Current max glob results: 5000.

items.description: Whether matching should be case-sensitive.
Optional maximum number of matches to return. Omit to use the configured server limit.
Optional file, directory, glob, or list of them that scopes the high-level search; omit to search the workspace root.
Text or regular expression pattern to search for; prefer built-in search tools so matches carry grounding metadata.
Whether query is interpreted as a regular expression.
8-character alphanumeric agent/workspace session_id returned by session_start.

rows: Search code content for matching lines. Use this tool for content discovery so results carry grounding metadata. pattern is text or regex depending on regex; paths scopes to files, directories, or globs. Results include numbered match lines, grouped context, snapshot metadata, and displayed ranges that can ground edit_lines. Current max_grep_results=200.

items.description: Directory path to render as a compact tree. Relative paths resolve inside the agent/workspace session workdir.
Maximum directory nesting depth to include in the tree view.
Maximum number of tree entries to return before reporting truncation. Bounded by server configuration.
8-character alphanumeric agent/workspace session_id returned by session_start.

rows: Return a compact directory tree inside an explicit agent/workspace session for high-level project orientation before targeted file reads. Pass the session_id returned by session_start. cwd defaults to the session workdir; any relative cwd override resolves inside that session workdir. Remote sessions dispatch to the paired worker session. Current max tree entries: 5000.

sections.heading: Secret Scan

items.description: Directory to scan. Relative paths resolve inside the agent/workspace session workdir.
Optional glob pattern to narrow which files are scanned.
Maximum number of findings to return before reporting truncation. Bounded by server configuration.
8-character alphanumeric agent/workspace session_id returned by session_start.

rows: Scan text files under an explicit agent/workspace session for common secret-like strings before commit, push, release, or sharing logs. Results are heuristic and do not prove the workspace is secret-free. Current max findings: 200.

sections.heading: Session

items.description: 8-character alphanumeric agent/workspace session_id returned by session_start.
Working directory to bind to the session. Local paths resolve inside the configured workspace; remote paths resolve on the selected worker.

rows: Change an existing local agent/workspace session to a new required workdir, clear stale grounding snapshots for that session, and return refreshed orientation metadata including instruction file paths. Use this when the user redirects you to a different project/subdirectory or you infer the original workdir was wrong; then read any relevant AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md/config files before continuing edits.

items.description: Optional human-readable label for this agent session.
Required remote worker machine name when target is remote. Omit for local sessions.
Session target. Use local for this workspace or remote for an online worker.
Working directory to bind to the session. Local paths resolve inside the configured workspace; remote paths resolve on the selected worker.

rows: Start an explicit agent/workspace session and bind it to a required workdir. Use target="local" for the control-server workspace, or target="remote" with machine set to an online remote worker name and workdir set to the worker-side directory. Before calling, ask the user which project directory or remote worker to use when unclear; otherwise infer the most specific safe workdir from the task, repository, or paths the user mentioned. For local sessions, the response includes discovered instruction file paths; read relevant AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md/config files before editing. Pass the returned 8-character session_id to read, search, edit_lines, bash, job, and other session-bound tools; remote sessions dispatch those normal tools to their paired worker session.

sections.body: Register shell execution and persistent-shell companion tools.
sections.heading: Shell

items.description: Persistent shell_id returned by bash(pty=true) or list_persistent_shells. This is not the agent/workspace session_id.

rows: Terminate a persistent shell by shell_id. Use for manually managed shells started with bash(pty=true), such as servers, watches, REPLs, or stuck interactive commands. shell_id is separate from the agent/workspace session_id. For tracked non-interactive jobs from bash(async_=true), use job(cancel=[...]) so the job record is updated. This is destructive for that shell process but does not delete files.
none

List active persistent shells created by bash(pty=true). Use this when you need the shell_id before reading, sending input, or killing a manually managed shell. The returned shell_id values are persistent-shell handles, not agent/workspace session_id values; async bash jobs are listed with job(session_id, list_jobs=true) instead.

items.description: Number of recent terminal lines to capture from the persistent shell.
Persistent shell_id returned by bash(pty=true) or list_persistent_shells. This is not the agent/workspace session_id.

rows: Read recent output from a persistent shell created by bash(pty=true). Pass shell_id returned by bash PTY mode or list_persistent_shells; shell_id is separate from the agent/workspace session_id. Use after send_persistent_shell_input to inspect an interactive or manually managed shell without blocking. For tracked non-interactive jobs from bash(async_=true), use job(poll=[...]) because it works from job_id and refreshes job status. lines defaults to 200 and controls how many recent terminal lines are returned; increase it only when needed for context.

items.description: Whether to start long-running non-interactive work as a tracked background job owned by this session.
Complete Python source code to write to a temporary script and execute.
Optional working directory for the command, resolved inside the agent session workdir. Omit or pass . to use the session workdir.
Optional environment variables for multiline or quote-heavy values; reference them from the command.
Optional combined stdout/stderr byte budget for bounded command mode.
Optional name for the tracked job or persistent shell.
Whether to start the command in a persistent PTY shell for interactive programs, REPLs, servers, or commands needing later input. PTY mode returns shell_id for persistent-shell companion tools.
Optional short purpose explaining why this tool call is being made. Maximum 500 characters.
8-character alphanumeric agent/workspace session_id returned by session_start.
Optional timeout in seconds for bounded command mode.

rows: Write Python code to a temporary file and execute it inside an explicit agent/workspace session. Pass the session_id returned by session_start. This is a convenience wrapper over bash that runs `python3 <temporary-script>` and supports the same cwd, timeout_s, max_output_bytes, env, async_, pty, and name controls. cwd defaults to the session workdir; any cwd override resolves inside that session workdir. Default mode is bounded and returns captured stdout/stderr under result. Set async_=true for a non-interactive background job owned by the same session_id and managed with job. Set pty=true only when the Python process needs an interactive terminal, returning shell_id for persistent-shell companion tools. Current bounded command timeout default/cap: 10/60 seconds.

items.description: Whether to send Enter after the input text.
Text to send to the persistent shell.
Persistent shell_id returned by bash(pty=true) or list_persistent_shells. This is not the agent/workspace session_id.

rows: Send input to an existing persistent shell created by bash(pty=true). Pass shell_id returned by bash PTY mode or list_persistent_shells; shell_id is separate from the agent/workspace session_id. Use this for interactive programs, REPLs, prompts, and manually managed server shells that need later input. Jobs started with bash(async_=true) are non-interactive background jobs; use the job companion for those instead. Set enter=false only when intentionally sending partial input without a newline.

items.description: 8-character alphanumeric agent/workspace session_id returned by session_start.

Replacement todo list. Each item may include id, content, status, and priority.

rows: Replace the current structured agent todo list for one session.

sections.heading: Version
rows: none
Return package and runtime version information.

sections.body: Register the special read-only search/fetch surface for connector clients. These tools are intentionally separate from the richer coding-agent file and search tools. Regular ChatGPT custom connectors and Deep Research-style clients often expose only a document-source pattern: search for result cards, then fetch one result by id. They may not surface general-purpose tools such as code search, file reads, shell commands, patches, or remote-worker operations unless the client is in Developer Mode or otherwise supports the full MCP tool set. search/fetch therefore need three special MCP-facing choices: 1. mcp_security_profile="connector_compatible" advertises a client-facing noauth-or-oauth securitySchemes profile for connector discovery. This is metadata only; AuthMiddleware still enforces real HTTP/MCP auth. 2. annotations="read_only" marks the tools as non-mutating document-source operations. 3. Their typed return models keep the connector-facing search/fetch payloads in the exact document-source shape expected by connector clients.
sections.heading: Read-only connector tools

items.description: Workspace result id returned by connector-style search, normally a relative file path.

rows: Fetch one workspace UTF-8 text file as a connector-compatible document.

items.description: Case-insensitive literal text query used by connector-style workspace search.

rows: Connector-style workspace document search. Search workspace text files and return connector-compatible result cards. Use this for connector-style document retrieval clients that expect a search -> fetch workflow. For code navigation or precise workspace inspection, use tools such as search, glob_search, tree_view, or read. Parameter: query is a case-insensitive literal text query. The tool searches from the workspace root, returns at most one result card per matched file, and each card id is the value to pass to fetch.

Pass the session_id returned by session_start. cwd defaults to the session workdir; any cwd override resolves inside that session workdir. Prefer the `cwd` argument over embedded directory changes; use `env` for multiline or quote-heavy values. Default mode is bounded and returns captured stdout/stderr. Set `async_=true` for long-running non-interactive work; this returns a job_id owned by the same session_id and must be managed with the `job` companion. Set `pty=true` only for interactive programs, REPLs, servers, or commands that need later input; this returns a shell_id for persistent-shell companion tools. Do not use shell_id with `job`, and do not use session_id with persistent-shell companion tools. If both async_ and pty are true, PTY mode is used. Use built-in `search` for source/content discovery so output carries edit-grounding metadata. Current bounded command timeout default/cap: 10/60 seconds.

tools.description:
Inspect, read output from, stop, or retry async bash jobs owned by an explicit agent session.

Results for `bash(async_=true)` are tracked by job id under the same session_id. Reach for this companion tool only when you need to intervene: list running work in the session, inspect recent output, stop stalled work, or retry a finished command. Starting work belongs in `bash`, not `job`.

Pass the session_id from session_start. Call with no action, or with `list_jobs=true`, to list jobs owned by that session. Use `poll=[id]` to inspect recent output/status for specific jobs, `cancel=[id]` to stop jobs, or `retry=[id]` to restart jobs with their original command and working directory. Do not combine actions in one call.

cancel.description: Tracked bash job ids in this session to stop because they are stalled, hung, or no longer needed.
cancel.title: Cancel

include_finished.description: Whether to include terminal jobs with exited, stopped, or lost status. Set false to show only active jobs.
include_finished.title: Include Finished

lines.description: Number of recent terminal lines to capture for a tracked job. Output is available only while the background job can still be inspected.
lines.title: Lines

list_jobs.description: Whether to return a snapshot of tracked bash jobs owned by this session. Omit all other job actions to list by default.
list_jobs.title: List Jobs

poll.description: Tracked bash job ids in this session whose latest output/status should be inspected. Use only when you need to check specific async bash work.
poll.title: Poll

retry.description: Tracked bash job ids in this session to restart with their original command and working directory.
retry.title: Retry

session_id.description: 8-character alphanumeric agent/workspace session_id returned by session_start.
session_id.title: Session Id

job_id.title: Job Id

last_started_at.title: Last Started At

session_id.title: Session Id

tools.description: Terminate a persistent shell by shell_id. Use for manually managed shells started with bash(pty=true), such as servers, watches, REPLs, or stuck interactive commands. shell_id is separate from the agent/workspace session_id. For tracked non-interactive jobs from bash(async_=true), use job(cancel=[...]) so the job record is updated. This is destructive for that shell process but does not delete files.

tools.description: Read recent output from a persistent shell created by bash(pty=true). Pass shell_id returned by bash PTY mode or list_persistent_shells; shell_id is separate from the agent/workspace session_id. Use after send_persistent_shell_input to inspect an interactive or manually managed shell without blocking. For tracked non-interactive jobs from bash(async_=true), use job(poll=[...]) because it works from job_id and refreshes job status. lines defaults to 200 and controls how many recent terminal lines are returned; increase it only when needed for context.

tools.description: Run compact remote control-plane actions. Use action="list" to discover worker names; action="invite" to create a one-time join command; action="revoke" to remove a stale or untrusted worker; and action="rename" to give a worker a stable name. Defaults: invite ttl_s defaults to the configured remote_invite_ttl_s=600 seconds when omitted. Security: treat invite output as sensitive because it grants enrollment capability.

action.description: Remote control-plane action: invite, list, revoke, or rename workers.
action.title: Action

args.description: Action-specific control-plane arguments. invite accepts name, workdir, ttl_s; list accepts {}; revoke accepts machine; rename accepts machine and new_name.
args.title: Args

action.description: Remote control-plane action requested by the caller.
action.title: Action

data.description: Structured output returned by the control-plane action.

tools.description: Write Python code to a temporary file and execute it inside an explicit agent/workspace session. Pass the session_id returned by session_start. This is a convenience wrapper over bash that runs `python3 <temporary-script>` and supports the same cwd, timeout_s, max_output_bytes, env, async_, pty, and name controls. cwd defaults to the session workdir; any cwd override resolves inside that session workdir. Default mode is bounded and returns captured stdout/stderr under result. Set async_=true for a non-interactive background job owned by the same session_id and managed with job. Set pty=true only when the Python process needs an interactive terminal, returning shell_id for persistent-shell companion tools. Current bounded command timeout default/cap: 10/60 seconds.

async_.description: Whether to start long-running non-interactive work as a tracked background job owned by this session.
async_.title: Async

code.description: Complete Python source code to write to a temporary script and execute.
code.title: Code

cwd.description: Optional working directory for the command, resolved inside the agent session workdir. Omit or pass . to use the session workdir.
cwd.title: Cwd

env.description: Optional environment variables for multiline or quote-heavy values; reference them from the command.
env.title: Env

max_output_bytes.description: Optional combined stdout/stderr byte budget for bounded command mode.
max_output_bytes.title: Max Output Bytes

name.description: Optional name for the tracked job or persistent shell.
name.title: Name

pty.description: Whether to start the command in a persistent PTY shell for interactive programs, REPLs, servers, or commands needing later input. PTY mode returns shell_id for persistent-shell companion tools.
pty.title: Pty

purpose.description: Optional short purpose explaining why this tool call is being made. Maximum 500 characters.
purpose.title: Purpose

session_id.description: 8-character alphanumeric agent/workspace session_id returned by session_start.
session_id.title: Session Id

timeout_s.description: Optional timeout in seconds for bounded command mode.
timeout_s.title: Timeout S

tools.name: run_python_code

tools.description: Search code content for matching lines. Use this tool for content discovery so results carry grounding metadata. pattern is text or regex depending on regex; paths scopes to files, directories, or globs. Results include numbered match lines, grouped context, snapshot metadata, and displayed ranges that can ground edit_lines. Current max_grep_results=200.

tools.description: Send input to an existing persistent shell created by bash(pty=true). Pass shell_id returned by bash PTY mode or list_persistent_shells; shell_id is separate from the agent/workspace session_id. Use this for interactive programs, REPLs, prompts, and manually managed server shells that need later input. Jobs started with bash(async_=true) are non-interactive background jobs; use the job companion for those instead. Set enter=false only when intentionally sending partial input without a newline.

tools.description: Start an explicit agent/workspace session and bind it to a required workdir. Use target="local" for the control-server workspace, or target="remote" with machine set to an online remote worker name and workdir set to the worker-side directory. Before calling, ask the user which project directory or remote worker to use when unclear; otherwise infer the most specific safe workdir from the task, repository, or paths the user mentioned. For local sessions, the response includes discovered instruction file paths; read relevant AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md/config files before editing. Pass the returned 8-character session_id to read, search, edit_lines, bash, job, and other session-bound tools; remote sessions dispatch those normal tools to their paired worker session.

label.description: Optional human-readable label for this agent session.
label.title: Label

machine.description: Required remote worker machine name when target is remote. Omit for local sessions.
machine.title: Machine

target.description: Session target. Use local for this workspace or remote for an online worker.
target.title: Target

workdir.description: Working directory to bind to the session. Local paths resolve inside the configured workspace; remote paths resolve on the selected worker.
workdir.title: Workdir

tools.name: session_start

GitSessionInfo.description: Lightweight git orientation for a session workdir.

branch.description: Current branch or short commit name, if available.
branch.title: Branch

dirty.description: Whether git reports uncommitted changes, if available.
dirty.title: Dirty

is_repo.description: Whether the session workdir is inside a git repository.
is_repo.title: Is Repo

GitSessionInfo.title: GitSessionInfo

created_at.description: Unix timestamp when the session was created.
created_at.title: Created At

expires_at.description: Optional Unix timestamp when the session expires.
expires_at.title: Expires At

git.description: Lightweight git orientation for the session workdir.

instruction_files.description: Workspace-relative project instruction files discovered near the session workdir.
instruction_files.title: Instruction Files

machine.description: Remote worker name for remote sessions.
machine.title: Machine

message.description: Short model-facing instruction for using this session.
message.title: Message

session_id.description: 8-character alphanumeric agent/workspace session id.
session_id.title: Session Id

target.description: Execution target bound to this session.
target.title: Target

updated_at.description: Unix timestamp when the session was last touched.
updated_at.title: Updated At

workdir.description: Canonical workdir bound to this session.
workdir.title: Workdir

workspace_root.description: Configured local workspace root for this server.
workspace_root.title: Workspace Root

tools.description: Return a compact directory tree inside an explicit agent/workspace session for high-level project orientation before targeted file reads. Pass the session_id returned by session_start. cwd defaults to the session workdir; any relative cwd override resolves inside that session workdir. Remote sessions dispatch to the paired worker session. Current max tree entries: 5000.

cwd.description: Directory path to render as a compact tree. Relative paths resolve inside the agent/workspace session workdir.
cwd.title: Cwd

depth.description: Maximum directory nesting depth to include in the tree view.
depth.title: Depth

max_entries.description: Maximum number of tree entries to return before reporting truncation. Bounded by server configuration.
max_entries.title: Max Entries

session_id.description: 8-character alphanumeric agent/workspace session_id returned by session_start.
session_id.title: Session Id

tools.name: tree_view

entries.description: Indented tree entries relative to root.
entries.title: Entries

exists.description: Whether the requested tree root exists.
exists.title: Exists

is_directory.description: Whether the requested root is a directory.
is_directory.title: Is Directory

message.description: Optional diagnostic message for missing or non-directory roots.
message.title: Message

nearest_existing_parent.description: Nearest existing parent for a missing root, when available.
nearest_existing_parent.title: Nearest Existing Parent

nearest_parent_entries.description: Entries in the nearest existing parent for a missing root.
nearest_parent_entries.title: Nearest Parent Entries

root.description: Resolved root path used for the tree view.
root.title: Root

truncated.description: Whether additional entries were omitted due to limits.
truncated.title: Truncated

tools.description: Return package and runtime version information.
tools.name: version

tools.description: Connector-style workspace document search. Search workspace text files and return connector-compatible result cards. Use this for connector-style document retrieval clients that expect a search -> fetch workflow. For code navigation or precise workspace inspection, use tools such as search, glob_search, tree_view, or read. Parameter: query is a case-insensitive literal text query. The tool searches from the workspace root, returns at most one result card per matched file, and each card id is the value to pass to fetch.

query.description: Case-insensitive literal text query used by connector-style workspace search.
query.title: Query

tools.name: workspace_search

id.description: Workspace result identifier passed to fetch.
id.title: Id

url.description: Connector URL for the result card.
url.title: Url

tools.description: Write a complete UTF-8 file inside an explicit agent/workspace session. Use for new files or intentional whole-file replacement. For precise modifications to existing files, prefer grounded `edit_lines`; use patch tools only when clearer. Do not replace an existing file wholesale unless that is the intended edit. Current write cap: 5000000 bytes.

content.description: Complete UTF-8 text content to write to the target file.
content.title: Content

overwrite.description: Whether to replace an existing file. Set false to fail when the target already exists.
overwrite.title: Overwrite

path.description: Workspace-relative path, or an allowed absolute path, for the file or directory operation.
path.title: Path

session_id.description: 8-character alphanumeric agent/workspace session_id returned by session_start.
session_id.title: Session Id

tools.name: write_file

created.description: Whether the file did not exist before this write.
created.title: Created

path.description: Workspace-relative file path that was written.
path.title: Path

tools.description: Replace the current structured agent todo list for one session.

session_id.description: 8-character alphanumeric agent/workspace session_id returned by session_start.
session_id.title: Session Id

todos.description: Replacement todo list. Each item may include id, content, status, and priority.
todos.title: Todos

tools.name: write_todos

content.description: Todo text shown to the agent.
content.title: Content

priority.description: Todo priority label, such as low, medium, or high.
priority.title: Priority

status.description: Todo status, such as pending, in_progress, or completed.
status.title: Status

updated_at.description: Unix timestamp when the todo list was last written, when known.
updated_at.title: Updated At
